title: Lard, Poultry Fats, and Drippings
content:
LARD

Rendered pork fat. Leaf lard (kidney fat) superior for flaky pastries. Hydrogenated lard better for cakes. Store covered in refrigerator.

Substitution: Use 15-20% less lard than butter.

POULTRY FATS

Chicken, turkey, duck, goose fats. Rendered leaf fat is firm, bland, light-colored. Store covered, refrigerated.

Substitution: ¾ cup clarified poultry fat = 1 cup butter.

DRIPPINGS

Fats rendered from cooking meats. Clarify before storage: heat slowly with potato slices (4-5 per cup fat) until potato browns, then strain. Store refrigerated.

Substitution: Use 15-20% less drippings than butter.

CLARIFYING FATS

Heat used frying fats slowly with 4-5 slices raw potato per cup. When potato is brown, strain through cheesecloth while warm. Store refrigerated.

MEASURING BULK FATS

Displacement method: To measure ½ cup fat, put ½ cup water in measuring cup, add fat until water reaches 1-cup mark. Drain water; remaining fat equals ½ cup.
